entry.title: Query for Prodution order nº, Receipt from Production (SI), Issue for Production(SO)
entry.content:
Hello everyone. Can you please help me to create a Query that contains the Receipt from Production (SI) and the Issue for Production (SO), for each production order?

It is also important that you can filter by date.

The objective is to classify the consumption of raw materials by business area (Distr. Rule), which we already have at the sales level, and now we want to go backwards to recover the data for 2024 at the cost level.

The ideal would be to create a table with a structure similar to this:

Prodution order nº / Receipt from Production (SI) / Issue for Production(SO)

Many thanks.

SAP Business One #SAP B1

entry.title: Re: Elevating Internal Transfers Transitions through SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding for Internal Hire
entry.content:
Hi @Farook : We implemented a solution to overcome this but we removed crossboarding from the process. you can refer the solution here - Internal Hiring: Recruiting-to-Employee Central - SAP Community

Please feel free to reach me out if you have any questions. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Compensation portelt not updated after Crossboarding process (ONB2.0)
Re: Compensation portelt not updated after Crossboarding...
entry.content:
Hi @Farook :

You can overcome this by implementing RCM to EC without crossboarding. We had multiple other issues during the crossboarding. We implemented the solution from this blog Internal Hiring: Recruiting-to-Employee Central - SAP Community and it worked. We went live with this solution.

Please let me know if you have any questions!

Thanks

Shruti

entry.title: Where can i start learning functions and customizing settings of pricing within SAP S/4HANA Sales?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Pricing in SAP S/4HANA Sales learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the functions and customizing settings of pricing within SAP S/4HANA Sales.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, application consultants and business users will be able to customize and use pricing for SAP S/4HANA Sales, ensuring efficiency and accuracy in sales pricing mechanisms.
Participants will be able to  maintain condition records, will learn to exploit special pricing functions and condition types, including tax determination, and to analyze and adapt the configuration of pricing in their own SAP S/4HANA system.
They will also learn about other related topics like managing condition contracts effectively.

 Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Sales
Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: ASSET RETIREMENT OBLIGATION IN SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) is duty and responsibility of the company to fulfil after the end of contract for the asset use. Let's understand with one example - ABC Oil and Gas company took land from XYZ landlord to set up gas station or petrol pump for the 10 years. While company using the land/property, it will be damaged therefore company agreed that they will clean the land, remove the gas station and improvement the quality of land and bring back into original form and hand over the land/property to landlord after 10 years. The cost of cleaning/removal/improvement of the land is known as asset retirement obligation for the company and they will show in their balance sheet as ARO liabilities as per US GAAP. 

We need to implement below BC sets (refer OSS 2856996) that will provide full configuration. One can use t-code SCPR20 to activate BC sets.

BC Sets = 1. /SAI/BC_AIF 2. /FOM/ARO_OBLIGATION_USG_V2 3. /FOM/ARO_AP_USG_V2 4. ZFACE_FOM_ITEMS.ZIP

Whole ARO process can be bifurcate into five sections.

1. ARO configuration- Consultant primarily has to understand accounting integration between ARO and fixed asset accounting as well as value ID determination.

2. ARO creation and release - Once configuration is done, user need to create ARO from t-code /FOM/AM01 by providing asset class, cost objects, contract duration, costs etc. once ARO is created and released it will generate asset master, asset acquisition entry and one time posting automatically.

  ARO Asset acquisition entry = Asset a/c Dr // To Contra Account: Acquisition Value a/c, both of these GLs are picked from AO90 account determination.

Onetime Posting (OTP) = Contra Account: Acquisition Value a/c // To ARO Liability a/c, both of these GLs are picked from ARO BRF plus account determination setting.

3. Accretion Posting - This is monthly cashflow accrual posting from t-code /FOM/ACE_PERI_RUN. This will generate Accretion Expenses a/c Dr // To ARO Liability a/c, both of these GLs are picked from ARO BRF plus account determination setting. This will equal the present value of ARO with future value of ARO at the end of ARO contract.

4.Depreciation Charges - User need to follow straight line method as per US GAAP for depreciation calculation on ARO asset. Run depreciation monthly from t-code AFAB. Journal entry is Depreciation a/c Dr // To Accumulated Dep a/c, both of these GLs are picked from AO90 account determination. This will make ARO asset value and accumulated depreciation value equal at the end of ARO contract.

5.Settlement/Decommissioning ARO - At the end of the ARO contract, user need to pass settlement entry that is ARO Liabilities a/c Dr // To Cash a/c

In summary, Asset Retirement Obligation is beautiful concept provided in SAP S/4 HANA. This makes end to end ARO process easy and compliant.

entry.title: Maintain Translations for Developer Extensions in S/4HANA and BTP
entry.content:
The Maintain Translations app offers a simple way to translate developer extensions into other languages. Developer extensions refer to objects created via developer extensibility, such as Data Elements, Message Classes, Business Configuration Objects, Data Definitions, Metadata Extensions and more.

For more information about the app, check this SAP help link: SAP Help - Maintain Translations.

To learn the process for maintaining translations, visit this blog by Vijay. The process is applicable to both BTP and S/4HANA as mentioned below -

 

Additional Steps:

After following the steps in the blog, you may be prompted to enter a transport request when uploading and publishing translations. Ensure you have created a TR under your name, as it will be required during this step.

Once completed, the changes should reflect in the Fiori app when accessed in another language. However, you may encounter situations where the translations don't immediately reflect in the UI service.

Resolving Translation Reflection Issues:

After translating the CDS annotations of a metadata extension or data definition, it is essential to activate the object again in ADT. This can be done by making a trivial change, like adding and removing a space, and then reactivating it. This step must be performed after publishing the translation in the Fiori app.

Why is Activation Necessary?

Fetching the service metadata from the server involves 5 layers of caching mechanisms, at least two of which only react on (translation) changes if an internal timestamp is changed. So far only the activation of objects involved in the service stack are able to update this timestamp. Since the Maintain Translations application is external to the service stack, the combined effort required to enable a reliable automatic refresh of the translation data in the service metadata as part of the Maintain Translations publish would be quite high, hence this is still in pipeline for SAP to implement.

Due to how the translation data of metadata extensions and data definitions is handled in the transport system, this issue can only affect the development system and the quality / productive systems in which the translations are imported will always have the up-to-date service metadata.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Production order scheduling taking Same date for Basic start date and end date
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have maintained In house production time as 3 days but while doing production Order. System is taking same date for basic start date and end date.

But when I am maintaining standard value in routing, System changes dates.

Kindly suggest steps to follow to resolve this issue.

Regards,

Tejas .P

entry.title: E-Learning: SAP BusinessObjects BI - Hybrid Integration
entry.content:
I'm very pleased to introduce the availability of some new E-Learning tutorials focused on hybrid integration between SAP BusinessObjects BI and SAP Analytics Cloud (and coming soon SAP Datasphere).

Many customers already have a significant investment in SAP BusinessObjects BI, and are maybe not ready to fully shift to cloud analytics within the short term and would rather gradually take on a few use cases in SAP Analytics Cloud.

As per the SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction:

"SAP Analytics Cloud is SAP’s strategic analytics & planning offering and is changing the way our customers
run their business, by allowing them to analyze their data in context and to make better and faster
decisions. The integration of analytics and planning augmented by AI in one native cloud solution allows to
move from insight to action, brings analytics closer to the point of decision, and provides trusted insights from
customers’ most valuable data.

SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform (SAP BusinessObjects BI) equips thousands of
companies which reliably manage enterprise reporting use cases at scale. With upcoming versions following 4.3, available on premise and as managed cloud, your investments are safe beyond 2030.
We plan to release the first version of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 in Q4 2024 where we will continue the
modernization started in version 4.3."

 
This new E-Learning is ideal for customers who are considering the future, and would like to build out some use cases based on a live connection between SAP BusinessObjects BI Universes and Web Intelligence documents using the Direct Connection type from SAP Analytics Cloud.
Aimed at system owners/administrators to guide you through usage of SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect to bridge the two systems

Goals:

  • Create a live connection between SAP BusinessObjects BI Universes and Web Intelligence documents using the Direct Connection type from SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Enable SAML SSO between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BusinessObjects BI
  • Enable Hybrid User Management between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise


Before you begin:

This E-Learning is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
Before you can access the E-Learning link, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required with your S-User ID. There is no additional cost involved. 
A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

ACCESS NOW



*Coming soon I will update this blog with another new E-Learning focused on connecting SAP Web Intelligence to SAP Datasphere analytic models using OAuth 2.0 

  • Introduction to the topic – what are we setting out to achieve and why? What is the use case? What are the architecture components involved? Technical requirements?
  • Step 1 - Create an OAuth Client in SAP Datasphere
  • Step 2 - Create an Authorization Server Configuration in SAP BI Platform
  • Step 3 - Create an SAP BI OLAP connection to SAP Datasphere
  • Step 4 - Consume SAP Datasphere analytic models in Web Intelligence

 

Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

As mentioned above, this topic is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map for SAP Analytics Solutions. Compliment your e-Learning experience by joining the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
We are providing continued support and collaboration, along with further enablement materials such as Learning Journeys, best practice documents, webcasts, and e-learnings.
The Value Map is a great way for you to access to your service and support entitlements, along with further support and empowerment for SAP Data, Analytics and Planning solutions.

Join the SAP Analytics Solutions value map today.
* S user required


 

 

  • Practical Guide: How to build SAP Analytics Cloud stories using SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere Connectivity Series
  • SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation
  • E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Planning

entry.title: BATMAS Setup: Sending BATMAS to external system when a new field (to add) is updated
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I have a task to do. It's necessary to send BATMAS to external systems when a new field X (to be added) is filled once the production is over. This new field X is linked to the batch and it's not present into the BATMAS03 standard IDoc.

I'd like to ask you something.

Regarding IDoc structure, considering BATMAS structure, where the new field should be added?
Regarding IDoc, is it necessary to add any change pointers related to the new field?

The idea is that, once the material has been created, and the batch is created too, the new value X will be linked to the material's batch once the production is over.

Any idea on how to solve this?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP PostgreSQL Database import to pgAdmin with SSH Tunnel (Local Access)
entry.content:
@mertuytun The ssh tunnel was open. 
Seems like the steps to connect to Postgresql hosted on GCP servers in SAP BTP is different to the ones hosted on AWS. 

The steps mentioned worked fine for AWS hosted service. GCP hosted service expect a certificate to connect. I believe we might have to push the certificate in binary to GCP servers to connect.

entry.title: PEP process in SF TimeOff before the bookable end date
entry.content:
For a customer we have a leave type with Quota called Annual Leave. Employee will be entitled to get every year 32 days of quota annually.

Now customer has requested the below things for Annual leave.

1.  Employee should be able to apply the leave till Feb end next year - Which is Possible in SF by setting the bookable period to till Feb end.
2.  Employee should be able to apply leave for next year Jan and Feb by Dec 31st of present year from present existing quota – Tried to control via Business Rule, but none of them worked.
3.  After point 1 and 2 PEP needs to be executed on 1st Jan of next year to post 10 and below left out balances to new Time Account(year) bucket. - PEP cannot be executed before as bookable period in point 1 is till Feb end of next year nor interim rule can be used to run as there will be future dated leaves in Jan and Feb.

Can you provide the solution/options for Point 2 and 3.

entry.title: Re: Multiple email to ship to party
entry.content: Thanks for the answer. It is resolved using FM ADDR_COMM_MAINTAIN

entry.title: Re: Install HANA DP Agent on Linux, Error Segmentation fault (core dumped)
entry.content: Temp. solution: Do not build on an Arm based MacOS but on a Windows machine 🙂

Regards, Fernando Da Rós

entry.title: Re: Transport code of a query
entry.content: What did you check to say that "the changes are not there"? The code is regenerated only "after" online execution, see here: https://me.sap.com/notes/562704/E

entry.title: Re: Catalog Object Grant ACCESS Issue in HANA DB
entry.content: The HANA database object's creator is its owner with full privileges as I know. If the owner is unavailable, may the SYSTEM user can change ownership. But ensure the SYSTEM user has the necessary privileges to run commands.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Profit Center Hierarchy Display in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
If the profit center hierarchy isn't showing in S/4HANA report apps, may you can try these steps:

- Ensure it's activated in the "Manage Global Hierarchy" Fiori App.
- It should be assigned to the right company code or controlling area.
- If there are multiple versions, ensure you're viewing the correct one.
- Nodes should be correctly defined and linked.
- Profit centers should have correct master data.
- Try viewing the hierarchy in the GUI to check for issues.
- If imported from another system, ensure synchronization was successful.

entry.title: Re: Data locking in measures
entry.content: I'm not sure what example you need, but you can just add measure as locking dimension in data locking configuration screen, and then you shuld be able to lock 3 measures as part of standard functionality.

entry.title: Re: J_1BEFD_COUNTRY obsolete in S4HANA
entry.content: @fedaros , so 2410984 - [TDF] Removing tables from obsolete package, should be understood as move the table back to a non-obsolete package? (J1BA)

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Data Processing for UI Migration from SAP...
entry.content: May using SAP HANA Smart Data Access (SDA) or Smart Data Integration (SDI) to create virtual tables in your HANA Cloud DB linked to your On-Premise DB. If this works fine, that could allow data processing directly on your On-Premise DB without replication, but may have its own challenges.

entry.title: Re: J_1BEFD_COUNTRY obsolete in S4HANA

entry.title: How to Create Custom Fiori Tiles in SAP
entry.content:
Creating Fiori tiles in SAP is an essential task for consultants looking to enhance the user interface and streamline transaction processes. In this blog post, I will guide you through the step-by-step process of creating custom Fiori tiles for transactions, using a role as an example. This post will include both the creation of catalogs and spaces for the tiles, and I’ll share important tips to help ensure smooth implementation.

Introduction

SAP Fiori tiles provide an intuitive and user-friendly way to access SAP transactions. In this tutorial, we’ll be creating a Fiori tile for the IW38 transaction, which is accessible by the Y_MAINT_ELECTRICIAN role. The steps covered in this guide will take you through the process of creating the necessary roles, catalogs, pages, and target mappings.

Steps to Create a Custom Fiori Tile

1. Create Business Roles and Check Authorizations
Before creating the tile, ensure that you have defined your business role and that it has access to the necessary transactions. In this example, we are using the role Y_MAINT_ELECTRICIAN, which already has access to the IW38 transaction.

 

2. Create a Customization Request
You need to create a customization request in the DEV system, as this request will later be transported to the QAS and PRD clients.

 

3. Run Transaction /n/UI2/FLPCM_CUST
Open the SAP GUI and run the transaction /n/UI2/FLPCM_CUST.

    • Once inside, click on the “Open in Designer” button to start configuring the Fiori tile.

 

4. Assign the Request
In the designer, click the settings button to assign your previously created customization request.

 

5. Select Your Request
Find the customization request you created earlier and select it.

     

 

6. Create a Catalog
Now, let’s create the catalog for the Fiori tile. In the bottom left corner, click the “+” icon to add a new catalog.

    • Enter the title, description, and catalog ID.

        

 

7. View Catalog Details
After creating the catalog, you will see two buttons: Tiles and Target Mapping. These options are essential for further configuration.

 

8. Create the Tile
To begin creating your custom Fiori tile, click the Create Tile button. This will open a new window for tile configuration.

 

9. Choose a Tile Template
For this example, we’ll select the template for Static Tiles, which is commonly used for transactions that don’t require dynamic content.

 

10. Fill in Tile Information 
Now, it’s time to populate the tile with relevant data.

    • In the General section, enter the title, subtitle, keywords (use the transaction code here), and any icon you want to use.
    • In the Navigation section, specify the Semantic Object, Action, and Parameters. This information can be found on the fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com by searching for the transaction code.
    • Once all fields are filled, click Save.

 

 

 

 

11. Visualize the Tile
At this stage, you will see a visual representation of your custom Fiori tile.

 

 

12. Create Target Mapping
Now, switch to the Target Mapping tab and create a target mapping for your tile.

    • Click the Create Target Mapping button to proceed.

 

13. Enter Target Mapping Details
Similar to tile creation, you will need to enter the Semantic Object, Action, and Parameters. Additionally, check the appropriate boxes for device types (such as desktop, tablet or phone) to ensure compatibility. Save your changes when done.

 

14. Run Transaction /N/UI2/FLP
After the catalog is set up, you will need to create spaces and pages for the Fiori tile. Run the transaction /n/UI2/FLP and open the Manage Launchpad Spaces tile.

 

15. Create a Space
Click the Create button to define a new space and enter the necessary information. You can create a page from this screen as well. Don’t forget to assign your request number here.

 

 

16. Assign the Catalog and Space to a Role
Now that both the catalog and space have been created, assign them to your role. Run the transaction PFCG to open the role and add the catalog and space.

 

 

17. Edit the Launchpad Page
Run the Manage Launchpad Pages tile and navigate to the page you created earlier.

    • Find the page and click on Edit button.
    • You can add sections to the page, and by clicking the Add button, you can specify which section should contain your tile.

 

 

 

 

 

18. Final Tile Display 
Your Fiori tile is now complete, and you should see it displayed in the assigned section. Congratulations!

 

 

Conclusion

Now that you’ve successfully created a custom Fiori tile, you can transport the request and implement the tile within your SAP environment. Custom Fiori tiles are a great way to simplify the user experience, and this process allows you to tailor the SAP Fiori Launchpad to meet your specific business needs. If you encounter any issues during the process, double-check the authorizations and parameters to ensure accuracy.

Feel free to comment with any questions or additional tips related to Fiori tile creation.

entry.title: [BUG] one today question shows the answer from another question
entry.content:
I clicked this link "Re: Transport code of a query" in my notification feed

The link in the notification feed is exactly this one: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/transport-code-of-a-query/qaq-p/13875579/comment-id/56906#M56906

After clicking it, I obtain this web page, with question from Transport code of a query - SAP Community (today 2024/09/24) but the answer is taken from Solved: Cannot find proper SAPEXE and SAPEXEDB during inst... - SAP Community (2017). If you go to this latter question and click on "Go to solution" you get https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cannot-find-proper-sapexe-and-sapexedb-during-installing-solution-manager-7/qaq-p/428068/comment-id/56906#M56906.

As you can see, the common thing is that we have "comment-id/56906#M56906" in the two links, hence probably the mixing of the two questions.

EDIT / IMPORTANT: after refreshing the Notification Feed page, the link is now fixed (!?), it's https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/re-transport-code-of-a-query/qaq-p/13875643/comment-id/4885247#M4885247.

entry.title: Re: How to find average based on entries of transactional...
entry.content:
Hi @azm_azm 

Based on your scenario, you can get your expected result through the Advanced Formulas script below. 

 

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202409" TO "202412"

//Create a Variable member named #Qty_Flag within the Account dimension.
VARIABLEMEMBER #Qty_Flag OF [d/Account]

//If the Quantity account has data, set the value of the #Qty_Flag variable member in the Account dimension to 1.
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Quantity") != NULL THEN
	DATA([d/Account] = #Qty_Flag) = 1
ENDIF

//Aggregate the #Qty_Flag for each Product and assign to the Unassigned member of the Product dimension.
DATA([d/Account] = #Qty_Flag, [d/Product] = "#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = #Qty_Flag)

//Replace the value of Average Sales for unassigned products. The new value is calculated by dividing the Total Sales by aggregate value of the quantity flag.
DATA([d/Account] = "Average_Sales", [d/Product] = "#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Total_Sales", [d/Product] = "#") / RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = #Qty_Flag, [d/Product] = "#")

 

To get the number of transaction data, the existing quantity value is converted to the number "1" on the variable member and aggregated to calculate the average value based on the number of transaction data. 

In my testing model, I got the average value below. 

 

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: ERROR=CANNOT USE DUPLICATE TRIGGER NAME
entry.content: As I know, the error happens when the import process tries to create a trigger that already exists. This could be because the source and target schemas have triggers with the same name, or the import process isn't renaming triggers correctly. To fix this, check for duplicate trigger names before importing and rename or delete as needed. If the error continues, contact the support team to check, if this is a bug.

entry.title: Why does the system check for calculation procedure for customer country when doing billing?
entry.content:
Why does the system check for calculation procedure for customer country when doing billing? It gives below error if destination country doesn't have the tax procedure assigned in the spro settings.

calculation procedure is not specified in country

entry.title: Re: Hide the value of certain cells in ALV GRID (not talking about whole columns/rows)

entry.title: Re: RAP Application Previewing Error in VSCode: Cannot fi...
entry.content:
I have the same error : 

Please let me know if u get any infos.

C:\Users\X\projects\project1>cd "C:\Users\X\projects\project1"

C:\Users\X\projects\project1>npm run start

> project1@0.0.1 start
> fiori run --open "test/flpSandbox.html?sap-client=010&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#project1-tile"

info fiori-tools-proxy Using UI5 version 1.102.2 based on manifest.json
info fiori-tools-proxy Starting fiori-tools-proxy using following configuration:
info fiori-tools-proxy proxy: 'http://www-XX:480'
info fiori-tools-proxy ignoreCertError: 'false'
info fiori-tools-proxy backend: [{"path":"/sap","url":"https://XX:44380/","client":"010"}]
info fiori-tools-proxy ui5: [{"path":"/resources","url":"https://ui5.sap.com","version":"1.102.2"},{"path":"/test-resources","url":"https://ui5.sap.com","version":"1.102.2"}]
info fiori-tools-proxy debug: 'false'
warn backend-proxy-middleware Cannot find module 'keytar'
warn backend-proxy-middleware Require stack:
warn backend-proxy-middleware - C:\Users\XX\projects\project1 ode_modules\@sap\ux-ui5-tooling\dist\middlewares iori-tools-proxy.js
warn backend-proxy-middleware Could not "require('keytar')". Trying VSCode's copy
warn backend-proxy-middleware Cannot find module 'vscode'
warn backend-proxy-middleware Require stack:
warn backend-proxy-middleware - C:\Users\XX\projects\project1 ode_modules\@sap\ux-ui5-tooling\dist\middlewares iori-tools-proxy.js
warn backend-proxy-middleware Could not get hold of vscode

entry.title: Excel download issue for custom fields in smart table SAPUI5
entry.content:
We are using smart table of type Analytical table. We have defined analytical table with custom columns within smart table. For few custom columns, we have used formatter to manipulate the text. In UI data is coming correctly when we download the excel this column gives empty data. Data is getting populated in excel with actual odata but not the formatted data. How to fix this issue?

Could you please help.

entry.title: Re: how to transform xml from one format to other
entry.content: please help on above post

entry.title: Asset accounting
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

I have a requirement where in the fixed asset report we need to add some categories like direct addition, transfer to main asset from CWIP in Fixed Asset Report as currently we are doing it manually by checking each line items.

So is there a way to do this or will this be done with the help of ABAPer.

entry.title: Designing a Tailored Story Report for Onboarding/Offboarding Custom Tasks in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Creating a SuccessFactors Onboarding/Offboarding Custom Task Story Report involves gathering data on custom tasks created in the Onboarding and Offboarding processes. Here's a step-by-step guide to build this report in SuccessFactors People Analytics:

Reporting Prerequisites:Enable Story Report

  • Onboarding Admin Access
  • Enable Create Story and Share Reports to Groups & Roles permissions.
  • Enable the Create Story permission and select the schemas you want to use while creating report stories.
  • Enable schema-specific permissions to access data from the schema tables. Refer to the Schema Permissions Required for Using Story topic for more information.

Onboarding Prerequisites:

  • Custom Task (Using MDF) is created and available in the system.

  • Report Schema Configuration Entity is created under Manage Data

  • Sub-Domain and Is Root are defined under Report Object Configuration Entity for Custom MDF Task

Go to Reporting

  • In the Admin Center, locate the Reporting section.
  • Click on Reporting to create new report and view & edit existing created and assigned reports.

Creating Query

  • In the Reporting section,  click on the New to create a new report.

  • Always select Optimized Design Experience for better experience and click on Create.

  • You can see the available Onboarding Data Sets in the right side of the panel.

Note: On the right-side panel you can see “Exit Interview” and it is a custom MDF Task which was added into report schema using Manage Data.

  • You can choose data sets to build your query as per the data required to build the dashboard.

Note: Here we have selected “User” domain to pull the basic employee details such as Name, ID, Division, Department etc.

Also, to pull the response of exit interview questions we need to access Custom MDF using “User” query.

Save the query and click OK to jump into the story designer page.

Creating dashboard

  • Choose the Page Layout and Apply (You can select a predefined template or create a blank page).

  • Select “Canvas” from the type of page you want to create.

  • Insert a table using “Insert” icon.

  • Select the column from the right-side panel.

Note: These fields can be renamed using right side panel.

  • Add Filter to the table report.

  • Select the filter fields from dimensions and click on filter by member.

Save the story once done with all the changes.

Running the Report

  • Go to  Report Center.

  • Click on the Run button to execute the report.

Here is another example of offboarding/onboarding task status report.

  • To build the linked analysis between chart and table, Edit the report and click on three dots.

  • Select the filter interactions apply to and settings based on your requirement.

Conclusion

Creating a People Analytics report story in SuccessFactors Onboarding is about turning data into actionable insights that drive decision-making.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning MOOC - Modernizing Integration with SAP Integration Suite!
entry.content:
Dear Dhanvanttri,

Thanks for your question. Yes, you can find an integration package with the set of integration flows and a groovy script collection on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to setup your pipeline on Cloud Integration. We are currently working on supporting the pipeline concept during migration from SAP PO. This is planned for Q12025 as part of the Roadmap.

I hope this helps!

Regards

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: Why does the system check for calculation procedure f...
entry.content: Error: 'Calculation Procedure Not Specified in Country'? Check SAP SPRO settings! Ensure tax procedures are assigned for each country to determine applicable take McDVOICE Survey taxes and ensure compliance.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Important Notice - Final Steps in Discontinuation of Legacy Allocations
entry.content:
As part of our ongoing efforts to enhance user experience and improve our product, we are writing this blog to remind SAP Analytics Cloud users about the planned discontinuation of the legacy Allocations functionality.

What’s Changing? Since QRC2 2023, we have gradually moved the Allocations legacy functionality to Data actions, with the immediate benefits of a simplified and harmonized design time experience while allowing the Allocations execution as part of an end-to-end workflow. With the QRC1 2024 release of SAP Analytics Cloud, we introduced the first restrictions on using the legacy Allocation functionality (more info here). Then, in QRC3 2024, we automatically migrated all legacy Allocation steps and processes to Data Actions representations (more info here). We have previously communicated our strategy through different channels, including in-app warning messages. 

 

 

Next Steps: The next step in the discontinuation process will be part of the SAP Analytics Cloud QRC4 2024 release. At that point, the following functionality will be disabled:

  1. Run a legacy Allocation Process
  2. Run a legacy Allocation Step in Data Actions

To continue operations without any interruption for users, please transition to Data Actions to run Allocation processes.  

View-only access to legacy Allocations through the Allocation legacy designer will remain until SAP Analytics Cloud QRC1 2025.

How to Make the Switch: Depending on how your planning processes are structured, you may need to follow the instructions for either case A, case B, or both.

Case A. Run legacy Allocations

  1. Identify Legacy Usage: determine where users are still relying on the legacy functionality within the system, such as Stories and Analytic Applications.
  2. Update Objects: apply the Data Action representations of the legacy Allocations generated during the QRC3 automatic migration to the identified Stories and Analytic Applications. Alternatively, you can manually migrate the legacy Allocation processes and steps into Data Actions and use the newly created Data Actions instead of the automatically created ones.
  3. User Guidance: notify end users of the changes where applicable. This is important mainly for workflows where users run Allocation processes using the “Execute Allocations” functionality from the toolbar. Instead, they will start the Allocation processes from a Data Action added to the Story.

Case B. Run a legacy Allocation Step in Data Actions

  1. Identify Legacy Usage: determine the Data Actions that still contain a legacy Allocation step. Such Data Actions can only exist if you have imported content containing legacy functionality after the QRC3 automatic migration.
  2. Update Data Actions: open the Data Action legacy Allocation step and choose “Migrate Now”. Once the migration is completed, the Data Action legacy step will be replaced with the Data Action native functionality for Allocations. If you choose to Save the Data Action, there is no need to update dependent objects, such as Stories, where these Data Actions are consumed.

Data Action containing legacy Allocation

We appreciate your cooperation in transitioning to the Data Actions functionality and are confident you will find it a significant improvement. Please do not hesitate to reach out with any concerns or questions!

entry.title: Re: Handling GR Inbound Resource in PPDS RRP3 - Product view
entry.content:
Handling resources are configured as Calendar Resources and hence it capacity is not utilized in scheduling. Kindly check your resource master configuration and in the 'APO Resource' tab identify the resource with the correct resource type. Also, refer to SAP documentation on calendar resource. 

Next, resend the PDS with CURTOADV_CREATE with full transfer option.

entry.title: Negative stock on 916 storage type
entry.content:
At one of our clients there is stuck negative stock on 916.
This happened due to a TO confirmed with 0 actual quantity, then the GI was posted for the delivery. Several deliveries affected.
No TR for the materials.
No open TO for the materials.
The stock is listed as pick/putaway quantity - I believe this should not have happened, may have happened due to some buggy development.
LT01/LT10 does not work as it says stock is zero at the source. The delivery can no longer be processed.

Any solution suggestions that does not involve cancelling the whole process?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
@David_Chaviano  Hi, I am interested in SAP Activate methodology. I have completed learning journeys Exploring SAP Cloud ERP, Exploring SAP Activate - Tools and Exploring SAP Activate - Agile Project Delivery. I have extensive Project Management experience and want a free certification attempt for SAP Activate - Associate. This will enrich my knowledge and authenticate my project management skills for SAP Project Management. I am mid careers switcher and technologically under developed area. Looking for a your support and help to learn more about SAP and become a useful resource of SAP. 

Regards, 

Muhammad Rizwan

entry.title: Re: Production Order Settlement Error
entry.content:
Hi Dilay,

You can use the posting period parameter to achieve that: settlement period 7 and posting period 8.

I hope this helps.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: Re: Integration of Data Action and Multi Action into File Repository (Part II)
entry.content:
Hi @ChenNee ,

reg. "you can remove the newly created team if desired"
After customers fully adopt this change & set all right permission of the data action objects in folder repository, they can remove the system generated teams & roles if desired...

However, when trying to delete the team "DataActionRole_Viewer" following warning is shown without further info:

Is there any way for customers to check this specific folder dependency before proceeding?
Because we could not find such folder(s).

entry.title: Re: Transport code of a query
entry.content:
Did you create the Infoset in Global Area or Standard Area

Global Area - In the automatically generated transport request look for objects such as

  • R3TR AQQU user group+query
  • R3TR AQSG Infoset 

Standard Area - with report RSAQR3TR

  • LIMU TABU AQTDB contain the information
  • Look at the logs provided by the report when using it to generate TR and then, after release/import of TR, to generate the query related objects

entry.title: SAP TechEd Virtual: Your SAP Community viewing party | São Paulo
entry.content:
Junte-se a nós no SAP TechEd Virtual: momento de reunir a comunidade SAP para uma manhã de aprendizado e networking em nossa watch party party em São Paulo! Assista ao SAP TechEd Virtual junto a outros clientes, parceiros e time SAP, conecte-se com especialistas e mergulhe nas últimas tendências tecnológicas.

O que esperar:

  • Transmissão ao vivo de duas sessões SAP TechEd Virtual 2024
  • Oportunidades de aprender, compartilhar ideias e networking com comunidade SAP

Agenda:

08:30 - 09:30Check-In & Welcome Coffee
09:30 – 10:00Abertura
10:00 – 11:30Transmissão: SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
11:30 – 12:00Transmissão: Keynote recap + Community Welcome
12:00 - 12:30Fechamento e Networking

Inscreva-se até 4 de outubro para garantir sua vaga. Vagas limitadas!

Até lá!

SAP Brasil

***Registo válido apenas através desta ligação.***

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: @HFumey greetings! I am in the US based outside of Philadelphia. May I ask you area of interests and expertise? Please check out SAP TechEd Virtual in 2024 | Learning Zone in preparation for SAP TechEd October 8-9. Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: How can an independent consultant purchase a new 'red...
entry.content:
@Margit_Wagner

Hi Margit,

i created yesterday a new order using the link and instructions you provided. This morning i got familiar email from SAP Store Admin requesting validations documents:
In order to complete your order, we need to validate your company information.
 
Therefore, please provide us with any of the supporting documents listed below so that we can validate your account and process your order
The Acceptable supporting documents are:
 
Link to local government authority websites or registry confirming requested details.
Legally valid documentation (such as Corporate Incorporation Certificate)
Tax certificates/government certificate.

I did not answer yet.

Last time i provided my VAT tax certificate along with some background info. Then my order was cancelled by SAP Store Admin. I wrote then two times to ‚ Customer Service SAP Training and Adoption’ (TRAINING.GERMANY@SAP.COM), but they are appearantly not responsible as their mailbox is not monitored (This mailbox is no longer in use and any inquiries submitted here will not be processed).

I then created a ticket in the new learning support portal, but they routed only to the SAP Store Admin who then cancelled my order for the second time (after asking me for validation information for which i provided again my VAT tax certficate, but to no avail).

The communication with SAP Store Admin is a bit difficult as they never actually said why they would not accept my vat tax certificate. They only did send standard emails.

One issue i noticed after reading your response yesterday. When placing my first order on September 1st, i entered for ‚company’ my last name and ‚Beratung’ (to indicate i am not a private person, but use SAP Learning Hub for business purposes). When placing the order yesterday,  this was defaulted, i could not change this. Not sure if this term ‚Beratung’ confuses the topic for the SAP Store.
I also wrote last week to 'support.emea@sap.com' who could not help but suggested to look for help here in the community forum (which i then did)

How do you suggest to proceed with this?
Best Regards
Marcus

entry.title: Re: VAT in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

my latest information (July 2024) is that this is not supported by standard solution in S/4HANA Cloud Public edition. Customer has to implement a workaround.

Please see this Customer Influence Request and vote, if needed:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/271470/?section=sectionDetails

Regards,

Doreen

entry.title: Re: VAT in Public Cloud
entry.content: OSS = One Stop Shop

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Pune (October 2024)
entry.content:
Following the success of this year's Stammtisch 2024, we are excited to announce the inaugural SAP Inside Track Pune! Mark your calendars for October 19, 2024, as we gather SAP enthusiasts, experts, and professionals for a day filled with insightful sessions, networking, and innovation. Registration is now open, and we encourage you to reserve your spot for this exciting event!

What is SAP Inside Track?   
SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, architects, and leaders come together to share their knowledge and insights on various SAP-related topics. This grassroots initiative, organized by members of the SAP Community, provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on the latest SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!

Event Highlights

  1. Expert-Led Sessions
  • Keynote Speeches: Hear from industry leaders and SAP experts about the latest advancements in SAP technologies.
  • Technical Deep Dives: Participate in sessions that cover a wide range of topics, from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Cloud Platform, and everything in between.
  • Hands-On Workshops: Engage in interactive workshops designed to provide practical experience with SAP tools and solutions.
  1. Networking Opportunities
  • Meet the Experts: Connect with SAP professionals, developers, and consultants from various industries.
  • Community Building: Build relationships with peers who share your passion for SAP and technology.

Why Attend?

  • Expand Your Knowledge: Gain insights into the latest SAP technologies and trends.
  • Enhance Your Skills: Participate in hands-on workshops and technical sessions.
  • Grow Your Network: Connect with like-minded professionals and industry experts.
  • Get Inspired: Discover new ideas and innovative solutions that can transform your business.

Registration Details

Registration for SAP Inside Track Pune is now open! Don’t miss this opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP. 

Visit the Pune SIT Registration Page: SAP Inside Track (SIT) 2024

Note: This is an in-person event only.

Event Details

  • Date: October 19th, 2024
  • Venue: SAP Labs India, ITPP, Pune
  • Contact: For any queries, please contact us: sappunecommunity@gmail.com

If you want to be a Speaker check this out: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Pune 2024... - SAP Community

Join the Conversation

Stay connected and join the conversation on social media using the hashtag #sitPUNE.

We can't wait to see you at SAP Inside Track Pune 2024! Join us as we come together to learn, share, and innovate.

Thanks & Regards

SAP Community Pune Core Team

Regards, Fernando Da Rós

entry.title: Will Spotlight functionality will be deprecating from 1H 2025
SAP Analytics Cloud Performance OnePager
entry.content:
The Challenge of Fragmented Monitoring Tools

It is sometimes difficult to perform performance analysis in SAC. It is common practice for administrators to have to switch between our different monitoring tools, each with its own set of statistics, metrics, and visualisations. The lack of interoperability between these tools introduces further complexity to the process. For those without in-depth expertise in integrating and interpreting these diverse data points, valuable insights can be lost in translation, and issues may go unnoticed until they escalate into critical problems.

The inefficiency is evident: instead of focusing on resolving bottlenecks and enhancing system performance, administrators are forced to sift through a bouquet of performance indicators, slowing down their workflow and delaying responses to potential performance degradation.

Introducing a Centralized Performance Dashboard - Performance One Pager

We are pleased to announce the introduction of a centralised performance dashboard which is designed to address the challenges previously mentioned and provides a comprehensive, centralised solution for all relevant performance data, presented in a clear and organised visual format. This Performance One Pager consolidates key statistics from disparate tools into a unified view, eliminating the need for manual data gathering for administrators.

Consider a scenario where, instead of cross-referencing different performance Stories, all of the critical statistics - BI query response times, high Open Story durations, long running Data Actions and Data Management Import Jobs, and large Planning Versions - are available at a glance. This approach allows both experienced administrators and less technical team members to access the insights they need without having to navigate into the complexities of each tool.

The System Overview Performance One Pager

The new start screen of the System Overview will close this gap and display all available performance data that requires attention in a centralized manner. This new home screen is called Performance One Pager. The existing functions of the System Overview will remain, but will be displayed further down because they are not relevant on a daily basis.

The forthcoming releases, 2024.19 and 2024.Q4, will see the integration of the Performance One Pager automatically into the System Overview.

Highlights
  1. Expensive Scenarios
  2. Errors and Warnings
  3. Version Sizes
  4. Details
  5. Integration with existing Performance Tools
Expensive Scenarios

This panel shows KPIs for expensive scenarios where:

  • BI queries run longer than 10s, 20s and 30s
  • Stories take longer than 10s, 30s and 60s to open
  • Log ons take longer than 10s and 60s
  • Data actions and import jobs run longer than 10min

These KPIs indicate that source systems are overloaded, queries are incorrectly designed, users or reports have problems, data actions and import jobs consume too many resources.

The Open Details dialog provides additional information such as runtime history and changelog and scenario-dependent details to narrow down troubleshooting. From the details dialog, the usual performance tools can be pre-filtered to display all details.

The same concept and similar data are available for the Errors and Warnings and Version Sizes panels.

For those who are not already familiar with the SAC Administrative Toolset, please visit the following blogs for additional background information:

  • System Overview 
  • Performance Analysis Tool 
  • Backend Performance Statistics 
  • Data Action Statistics 
  • Data Management Job Statistics 
  • Private Version Statistics 
  • Error Messages Statistics

entry.title: How to view MVC in NWDS 7.5 for SAP EP(Portal) from SLD
entry.content:
Hi,

We migrated the SLD to Azure. I have never used NWDS. I have been asked to test if the development components are working fine. Can someone help me how to view the data in sld from NWDS 7.5 and how to deploy the changes to dev/qa system

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

My name is Katerina, and I’m excited to apply for the Digital Skills Initiative. After taking a 10-year break to focus on my family, I am now ready to return to work and start a new career. Coming back to work after so many years feels challenging, but I believe this program will help me gain the skills I need.

I am especially interested in learning about SAP technologies because I know they are used in many businesses around the world. I want to build a new career where I can help companies with digital solutions and a transition to cloud.

As a woman returning to work after a long break, I really appreciate the support this program offers. I am hopeful that with the skills I learn through this initiative, I can confidently start this new chapter in my life and contribute to the companies I will work for.

Thank you for considering my application. I am excited to begin this journey and learn new skills that will help me in my future career.

Sincerely,
Katerina

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi Stephanie
I have been working in ABAP for over twenty years. In the next months one of my goals is to catch up and learn new techniques and technology in depth and not just scratch the surface of what SAP has to offer.

My calendar is already marked with a couple of tracks during TechEd.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Pricing in V2 how to integrate with ERP and or upload price records in mass
entry.content: Hi, do you have any update in this topic?

entry.title: The Case for SAP BTP: The People’s Verdict
entry.content:
After a careful review of all the evidence submitted in the form of product reviews, G2 has announced the people’s verdict on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) in their 2024 Fall reports, and the news is good; SAP BTP has once again been identified by customers as a leader in the Cloud Computing Platforms, Cloud Platform as a Service (PaaS), Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), and Database as a Service (DBaaS) categories.

This outcome of course is great news for SAP BTP and consistent with prior judgements, which have been determined using a robust assessment methodology designed by G2, indicating a strong level of customer satisfaction with this technology platform. But the question could now be asked, what are the compelling features and outcomes that customers experience when using SAP BTP, which makes the case for this technology platform so strong? The answer to this question is possibly best brought to light by an examination of some recent product reviews, in the customers’ own words.

“It makes everything work together so easily…”

SAP Business Technology Platform is very helpful because it makes everything work together so easily. This makes managing complicated systems a lot easier. We can also connect different third-party apps, and that's possible with automatic processes and smooth data flow…SAP BTP helps us understand things in real-time, so we can make quick choices based on the most up-to-date data. It can be changed and scaled up or down to fit the needs of our business and save us money at the same time. Review from mid-market customer, 19 Sept 2024

“SAP BTP excels at integrating diverse systems…”

SAP BTP excels at integrating diverse systems, both SAP and non-SAP, across on-premise and cloud environments. It provides tools like SAP Integration Suite that facilitate smooth, real-time data flow between different applications, which is critical for businesses with complex IT landscapes. Review from small business customer, 17 Sept 2024

“…it brings everything together in one place…”

What I like most about SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is how it brings everything together in one place. It makes it easier for businesses to build new things or improve what they already have, without needing to switch between different systems. You can use simple tools to create apps, even if you’re not a tech expert, and that’s really helpful…In the end, it’s not just about technology—it’s about solving real business problems and helping companies grow, and that’s what makes it so useful. Review from mid-market customer, 16 Sept 2024

“…flawless integration proficiency and dynamic automation tools”

The thing I like the best about SAP BTP is its flawless integration proficiency and dynamic automation tools. The platform combines with both SAP and non-SAP systems so perfectly which makes it a great choice for my diverse tech environment. Its cloud native infrastructure is extremely useful for scaling and the security of this platform gives me opportunity to handle sensitive data. Also the customer support is very helpful for any issue/query. Review from enterprise customer, 7 Sept 2024

A continual focus on development and innovation

I can say with confidence, that this news is uplifting to all members of the SAP team involved in developing, innovating and supporting SAP BTP, who continually strive to make this platform and its associated cloud services the best they can possibly be for the customers who use them.

“Thank you” G2, for once more awarding SAP BTP with these wonderful Fall 2024 badges, as well as to our customers too for their kind words.

Until next time, thank you for reading and have a productive final three months of 2024, and if you get time why not take a few minutes to read some of our many other SAP BTP customer reviews.

entry.title: Re: Can Payment terms fix a date for the next few months?
entry.content:
Hi kent_luo

What I understood from your question that you want payment terms with fixed date. 

If it is the your question so Please go through the below blog it may help you https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/payment-terms-with-fixed-date/ba-p/13364187

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Creation of Additive Costs in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I am looking at creating Additive Costs by selecting the option "Cost Comp Struc w/ Texts" in SAP Public cloud via the application " Create Additive costs"

Can you please guide me with the steps and the necessary configurations needed for this purpose.

The intention is to to achieve COGS Split via Additive costing since my client is not a manufacturing organization and hence no BOMs are used

Request your valuable inputs

Regards

entry.title: Re: J_1BEFD_COUNTRY obsolete in S4HANA
entry.content: Or try SM30 on view J_1BEFDV_COUNTRY and navigate to Customizing

entry.title: Re: Error While Executing "Capacity scheduling table" App.
entry.content:
Hi @Prashanth2_D 

 

Have you created the Work Centers in the 'Create Work Center' app ?

 

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('CR01')/S33

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager - CICO Feature
entry.content:
Dear All, 

We are working on SAP Service and Asset Manager since long time and have done multiple upgrades from 2005 till 2310 . We are also live with 2210 since last 1 year and now upgraded to 2310 and live from few months

We have activated the WO assignment types as below

  • Assignment Type 2: Operation-level personnel number of the work order
  • Assignment Type 6: Operation- or task-level work center*

The requirement is that users can see all the orders from their work centers also they can be assigned operations from their / other work centers .

Also the major requirement is that "Multiple users can work on same operations" . For this reason we have activated the CICO feature 

Now when we say "Multiple users can work on same operations"  - This means that :

  • Multiple users can add time on the same operations
  • Multiple users can change the status of same operation- ISSUE

However the second point is giving a lot of trouble to us- especially with the 2310 My Work Mobile cards section

  1. At a time only one operation should be started clocked in and should give Hold and End Operation option - Some times the application shows Hold and complete options in all the operations
  2. Some times the buttons become non responsive ( CLock in , Hold , End Operation)
  3. Some times the button tool bar is invisible
  4. The status in mobile cards do not match with status in operation detail screen

We already have raised OSS for this and its work in progress with SAP

My intention to raise this question in forum is to learn if anyone else is also using the similar configurations and has similar requirements,

If Yes ,can you share your experience and your way around to solve this issue. 

This will be vey helpful for us and I am sure will be helpful to others as well.

Looking forward for your support

Thanks and Regards

Neha Mahanty

entry.title: Re: ASSET RETIREMENT OBLIGATION IN SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
There are very rare documents on ARO, this is a nice document. Thank You for sharing the knowledge.

Is there any other documents that explains the different fields and uses of them from ARO master data?

entry.title: How to hide transactions in MII?
entry.content:
Dear community

At a project with MII, I've seen that it's possible to hide transactions in a way that they cannot be viewed in the workbench but still be used through http requests.

How do you do this? Is this a parameter somewhere, is this with roles?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Fiori Element - failed to load 'sap/fe/templates/ObjectPage/SectionLayout.js
entry.content: did you find a solution to this? I'm getting a similar error when trying to run an application from local launchpad
